name: Te Papa
cultural_protocol: Respect taonga and tupuna guidance from source
notes: Live as a rights-aware preview-media onboarding pass. Metadata stays CC BY 4.0, item-level image rights remain attached, and culturally sensitive records should continue to be excluded by default.

name: Auckland Museum
cultural_protocol: Protect taonga, sensitive material, and source protocol flags
notes: Live in ARCHAI now. Source-side placeholder suppression is active in the harvester, and a formal terms re-check should still happen before bigger expansion.

name: DigitalNZ
cultural_protocol: Aggregator content may carry culturally sensitive legacy descriptions
notes: Excellent for breadth and Pacific/Aotearoa reach, but only safe if we filter by rights and keep partner provenance attached.

name: QAGOMA
metadata_rights: CC BY 4.0 (Queensland Government open data, CKAN CSV)
image_rights: metadata-only — per-object image API (collection.qagoma.qld.gov.au) is behind Cloudflare bot mitigation, returns HTTP 403 to automated requests
quality_gate: schema-stable + attribution-preserved (images excluded until API access arranged)
cultural_protocol: Review any Indigenous or culturally sensitive material in downstream records
notes: Strong Australian and Asia-Pacific balancing source. Metadata (CC BY 4.0) harvests cleanly via CKAN CSV. The per-object image API is Cloudflare-protected and blocks all automated requests (HTTP 403), so QAGOMA records are metadata-only and are skipped by the image-only AUX.IO page generator. Partnership outreach to the QAGOMA digital team for formal API/media access is the path to enabling images.

name: National Museum of Australia
image_rights: public domain or CC BY-SA / CC BY-NC-SA via API
cultural_protocol: Source warns that some material is culturally sensitive and may require permission
notes: Strong Australian object source, but its API terms are explicitly non-commercial. Fine for research/demo framing, not for any commercial-facing deployment.

name: WA Museum
quality_gate: verify terms + image reuse + collection stability
cultural_protocol: Expect Aboriginal and sensitive heritage material review needs
notes: Worth pursuing for regional and maritime balance, but legal and API details still need a proper source check.

name: M+
cultural_protocol: Standard museum sensitivity review; excellent multilingual potential
notes: Live in ARCHAI now. Public GraphQL metadata access and bilingual fields are working, and preview media is preserved with source-rights context attached.

name: Tokyo Museum Collection
cultural_protocol: Preserve Japanese and English fields cleanly; obey source-provider media terms
notes: Technically explored and multilingual-friendly, but currently held out of live onboarding because the public-rights gate did not yield a strong enough set of image-backed records.

name: National Palace Museum
quality_gate: verify open-data terms + image sizes + language fields
cultural_protocol: Expect multilingual complexity and object-level rights nuance
notes: Potentially strong for historic Asian collections, but needs a proper legal and technical pass before onboarding.

name: National Taiwan Museum of Fine Arts
quality_gate: verify open-data and media rules before public use
cultural_protocol: Preserve Chinese and English text cleanly
notes: Promising art source, but still waiting on a formal legal and technical check.

name: Brasiliana Museus
quality_gate: image-backed + source provenance + item-level rights spot-check
cultural_protocol: Strong diversity gain; keep local context and language intact
notes: Live in ARCHAI now. Onboarding is filtered to public-domain / open-access records that pass the item-level rights gate before public surfacing.

name: Qatar Museums
quality_gate: verify API access or structured export + media terms
cultural_protocol: High cultural sensitivity; provenance and respectful presentation are critical
notes: Potentially important Middle East source, but not ready until API and reuse terms are properly confirmed.

name: Qatar Digital Library
quality_gate: verify object/media reuse and whether record type fits ARCHAI object model
cultural_protocol: Archive-oriented material may need different UX treatment than object collections
notes: Valuable regional archive source, but likely better for document/knowledge layers than object-heavy AUX.IO first.

name: Computer History Museum
quality_gate: verify developer access and media reuse before harvest
cultural_protocol: Low cultural sensitivity risk, high relevance for media-tech history
notes: Excellent thematic fit for technology objects, but not yet legally or technically verified in this pass.

name: Trove
cultural_protocol: Legacy description and sensitivity checks required
notes: Useful discovery layer for Australian breadth, but better as metadata and reference enrichment than first-wave AUX.IO media source.

name: National Gallery of Art, Washington
quality_gate: openaccess=1 + image-backed + source provenance; exclude openaccess=0 fair-use derivatives
cultural_protocol: Retain source descriptions and review sensitive records independently of copyright status
notes: Daily NGA CMS export with stable object IDs, UUIDs, IIIF media, accessibility text, and a machine-readable item-level open-access gate. The harvester streams the large CSV files and never ingests images marked openaccess=0.

name: Smithsonian Open Access (SAAM + Cooper Hewitt)
image_rights: CC0 — Public Domain (filtered to usage.access=CC0 in API response)
cultural_protocol: Low cultural sensitivity risk; standard museum sensitivity review applies
notes: Covers Smithsonian American Art Museum (SAAM) and Cooper Hewitt National Design Museum (CHSDM) as priority units. CC0 is verified at item level via the API's usage.access field — only records flagged CC0 are harvested. Free API key from api.data.gov required. Massive balance gain for modern American art and design.

name: Tate Collection
image_rights: CC0 for public domain works (Tate Open Access 2019). Harvester restricted to pre-1920 works only.
cultural_protocol: Standard museum sensitivity review; no known Indigenous or restricted material
notes: Dataset harvested from GitHub (CC0). Images served from tate.org.uk CDN. Harvester applies a strict pre-1920 creation-year filter to ensure all works and their images are in the public domain. Covers Turner, Blake, Constable, Rossetti, Millais, Sargent, Whistler, and early European modernists held by Tate.

name: Street Art & Public Murals (multi-city open data)
metadata_rights: OGL Canada (Vancouver) · CC BY 2.0 (Brussels) · NYC Open Data / Public Domain (NYC) · CC BY 4.0 (Melbourne) · Public Domain (San Francisco) · Public Domain (Chicago)
image_rights: per-source: OGL / CC BY / public domain — all allow public display with attribution
cultural_protocol: Low cultural sensitivity risk; standard public art context
notes: Aggregates six city open-data portals: Vancouver Public Art (OGL Canada), Brussels Street Art Trail (CC BY 2.0), NYC Parks Art in the Parks (public domain), City of Melbourne Outdoor Artworks (CC BY 4.0), San Francisco Civic Art Collection (public domain), and City of Chicago Public Art (public domain). No API key required for any source. Geolocation data (lat/lon) preserved in payload for future map views. Source attribution attached per record.

name: Whitney Museum of American Art
image_rights: NOT CC0 — images of in-copyright works require separate licensing via Whitney Images & Permissions. Metadata-only harvest recommended.
quality_gate: metadata-only + no image display + attribution on all records
cultural_protocol: Standard museum review; 20th–21st C American art context
notes: CC0 covers metadata (artist names, titles, dates, mediums, dimensions) only. Images of Whitney collection works are NOT open access — most are in-copyright contemporary art. Can be harvested for semantic search and conversation layers without image display. Strong gain for 20th–21st C American art coverage in search results.

name: Museum of Modern Art (MoMA)
image_rights: NOT included in CC0 dataset. Images licensed via Art Resource (North America) or Scala Archives. Cannot display images without separate licensing.
cultural_protocol: Standard museum review
notes: 124K works as CC0 metadata on GitHub. No images available under open licence — images must be licensed separately through Art Resource. Valuable for enriching semantic search with modern/contemporary art vocabulary, artist names, and classification. Do not surface image fields from this source.

name: Harvard Art Museums
image_rights: Non-commercial use only. 2-week caching limit. Attribution required on every page.
cultural_protocol: Standard museum sensitivity review
notes: Rich provenance, exhibition history, and object verification fields. 200K+ objects. Non-commercial restriction means this is suitable for the ARCHAI research context but must be reviewed before any commercial deployment. API key free, rate limit 2500 req/day, 2-week caching maximum — do not store records indefinitely without re-checking terms.

name: Rhizome ArtBase
image_rights: Per artwork — net art works are typically web-hosted; screenshots/thumbnails need per-work review
cultural_protocol: Net art may contain content requiring review before public display
notes: The canonical born-digital art archive. 2,100+ net art, software art, browser art, and HTML works. Wikibase/SPARQL infrastructure since 2021 NEH CARES rebuild. Maps directly onto the ARCHAI Born-Digital & New Media tagging theme. Needs a dedicated SPARQL harvester and per-work media rights check before image display.

name: RAWG Video Games Database
metadata_rights: Free with attribution for non-commercial and small projects (<100K MAU)
image_rights: Attribution required — active hyperlink back to rawg.io on every page where data is displayed
cultural_protocol: Game content may require content-rating review before public display
notes: 300K+ games with metadata, screenshots, release dates, genres, platforms. Treats video games as cultural objects in the ARCHAI collection — a major modernising addition. Attribution hyperlink to rawg.io required on every page where data is displayed. Free for the ARCHAI research / demo context. Build harvester targeting landmark games by decade, genre, and cultural significance.

name: J. Paul Getty Museum
image_rights: CC0 1.0 Universal — Getty Open Content Program (~140,000 images). Harvested via the Linked Art SPARQL endpoint (data.getty.edu/museum/collection/sparql) + per-object CIDOC-CRM JSON-LD; IIIF image service. The legacy collectionapi.getty.edu endpoint was retired and is no longer used.
cultural_protocol: Low cultural sensitivity risk; ancient art objects should use respectful contextualisation for Indigenous or sacred objects
notes: The Getty Open Content Program releases ~140,000 high-resolution images as CC0 with no restrictions. Collection strengths: ancient Greek and Roman art, European paintings (Rembrandt, Rubens, Titian, Van Dyck), illuminated manuscripts, Italian drawings, 19th-century photography, French decorative arts. IIIF image API available for all open content. No key required.

name: Wellcome Collection
image_rights: CC BY 4.0 for majority of collection. Verified per-item via license.id in API response. Attribution required.
cultural_protocol: Medical imagery may include human remains, anatomical subjects, or historical treatments requiring sensitive framing; historical mental health material requires care
notes: Unique interdisciplinary collection at the intersection of art, science, and medicine. ~750,000 items including anatomical illustrations, wax models, scientific photographs, apothecary objects, pharmaceutical packaging, manuscripts, and art about the body and health. Wellcome Catalogue API v2 is free, no key required, JSON responses with IIIF images. Attribution required on all CC BY items.

name: Art Blocks (On-Chain Generative Art)
image_rights: Generated images: owned by token holders. Metadata and project descriptions are public. Static preview images require per-project rights check.
cultural_protocol: Standard contemporary art review
notes: Ethereum on-chain generative art platform. Project scripts, dependency libraries, and token hashes stored immutably on-chain. GraphQL available via The Graph subgraph. Image rights are complex — each minted token is owned by the collector, though project descriptions and scripts are public. Start with metadata-only harvest; confirm image display rights per project before surfacing media.
